mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed 55% when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or unemployment spikes fail to predict return direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in >50% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and business investment, which translates into higher near-term corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict 5-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.01 or returns are negative during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 255 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in >55% of high-inflation periods or inflation threshold shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative returns in >50% of observations or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, which systematically generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth shows a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are negative during expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts signals weakening construction activity and reduced capital expenditure, which forecasts lower near-term economic momentum and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing start declines fail to predict negative returns with statistical significance or positive returns exceed 50% of triggered instances.
